notes: [Verified 2026-03-17 | Surg Endosc 2006 | content_match] Morgenstern L. 'George Berci: past, present, and future.' Surg Endosc. 2006 May;20 Suppl 2:S493-4. Written by Leon Morgenstern, the Chair of Surgery at Cedars-Sinai who recruited Berci in 1967. Documents Berci's 'contributions to the art and science of endoscopy and laparoscopy' over 'nearly half a century.' Berci (1921-2024) was born in Szeged, Hungary, graduated University of Szeged 1950, fled during Hungarian Revolution 1956 on a Rockefeller Fellowship to Melbourne. In Australia he developed the first miniature endoscopic camera (1962). Recruited to Cedars-Sinai 1967 as visiting scholar, became director of the multidisciplinary surgical endoscopy unit ~1970, and remained active until his death at age 103. The program he founded was the first multidisciplinary surgical endoscopy division in the US. Berci goes from degree 1 (SAGES founder) to degree 2. This creates a West Coast institutional anchor for the MIS module — previously all US MIS nodes were Midwest/South (Ponsky/Cleveland, Reddick/Nashville).

notes: Gerald Marks identified as founder of SAGES per PMID 29362909. SAGES founded 1981. Added 2026-03-18.

target_node: Society of American Gastrointestinal and Endoscopic Surgeons
notes: The ACS integrates with SAGES to accredit advanced MIS fellowships and define the core credentialing framework for modern laparoscopic and robotic surgery.

[Phase2 replaced PMID: 10562635 -> PMID: 10444020 on 2026-03-17] [Verified 2026-03-17 | JSLS 1999 | content_match] Dubois adopted and modified the laparoscopic cholecystectomy technique after observing Mouret's initial breakthrough.

[Phase2 replaced PMID: 2682975 -> PMID: 10444020 on 2026-03-17] [Verified 2026-03-17 | JSLS 1999 | content_match] Reddick traveled to Paris to observe Dubois, subsequently performing early laparoscopic cholecystectomies in the US.

target_node: Society of American Gastrointestinal and Endoscopic Surgeons
notes: [Phase2 replaced PMID: 24002773 -> PMID: 18945707 on 2026-03-17] [Verified 2026-03-17 | Surg Innov 2008 | content_match] Reddick's introduction of laparoscopic cholecystectomy to the US was disseminated through SAGES meetings and training infrastructure, making SAGES the governance vehicle for the MIS revolution.

Marescaux founded IRCAD as a dedicated research and training institute for minimally invasive and robotic surgery. [Phase2 upgraded institutional_archive -> PMID: 11923603 on 2026-03-18] [Verified 2026-03-18 | Ann Surg 2002 | content_match] Marescaux J, et al. 'Transcontinental robot-assisted remote telesurgery: feasibility and potential applications.' Ann Surg. 2002 Apr;235(4):487-92. Marescaux's affiliation listed as IRCAD-EITS, Louis Pasteur University, Strasbourg. Confirms IRCAD as a research institute for MIS/robotic surgery founded and directed by Marescaux. Founding year (1994) documented in institutional sources.

target_node: Society of American Gastrointestinal and Endoscopic Surgeons
notes: Marescaux and IRCAD served as a primary international training hub integrated into SAGES educational programming, bridging European MIS innovation to the American governance framework.
